_methodology: cpupower frequency-set -g performance + intel_pstate/no_turbo=1, taskset -c 0, nice -20. 5 runs LTO, 3 runs no-LTO. bench_bitcoin -filter=<benchmark> -min-time=3000. Medians computed from all runs.

machine_spec.turbo: disabled (intel_pstate/no_turbo=1 + cpupower — hard lock, sudo available)

note: All numbers from Bitcoin Core bench_bitcoin binary — real Bitcoin Core validation pipeline. NOT bench_unified. Hard turbo lock: cpupower + intel_pstate/no_turbo=1. LTO: 5 runs, median. no-LTO: 3 runs, median. err% from nanobench per-run internal measurement.

results_lto.note: Release + LTO (-DCMAKE_INTERPROCEDURAL_OPTIMIZATION=ON) — recommended production build. 5 runs, hard turbo lock.

VerifyScriptP2WPKH_ns.verdict: Parity — Ultra 0.4% slower, ranges overlap, inconclusive

results_nolto.note: Release without LTO (-O3, no -flto). 3 runs, hard turbo lock. HONEST: Ultra is ~0.5-1.0% SLOWER than libsecp without LTO. PERF-002 reduced the gap from ~1.1% (pre-fix) to ~0.5-1.0% (post-fix) but did not eliminate it. Use LTO for production Bitcoin Core builds.
results_nolto.build: Release (no LTO), GCC 14.2.0. build-ultra-release-nolto vs build-libsecp-release-nolto.

test_suite.note: 749/749 Bitcoin Core unit tests pass with Ultra backend

nolto_gap_root_cause.status: PARTIALLY_RESOLVED — PERF-002 reduced gap from ~1.1% to ~0.5-1.0%, but not eliminated
nolto_gap_root_cause.original_finding: ConnectBlock without LTO was slower due to Ultra's larger instruction footprint (~1.3 MB vs libsecp ~400 KB) creating i-cache pressure, plus a redundant y²=x³+7 on-curve check in ecdsa_verify.
nolto_gap_root_cause.perf002_fix: Removed redundant y²=x³+7 on-curve check from secp256k1_ecdsa_verify (~400ns per call). Reduced the no-LTO deficit from ~1.1% to ~0.5-1.0%. The i-cache pressure from the larger code footprint remains.
nolto_gap_root_cause.fresh_run_result_2026_05_12: no-LTO measured 2026-05-12 with hard turbo lock: AllEcdsa 0.5% slower, AllSchnorr 1.0% slower, Mixed 0.8% slower. All non-overlapping, all confirmed slower.
nolto_gap_root_cause.root_cause: Ultra's larger code footprint (~1.3 MB secp256k1 symbols vs libsecp ~400 KB) creates i-cache pressure without LTO. LTO resolves this by co-optimizing code layout globally. PERF-002 removed a secondary contributor (~400ns redundant check) but the primary i-cache pressure remains.
nolto_gap_root_cause.recommendation: Use Release+LTO for production Bitcoin Core builds. Without LTO, Ultra is ~0.5-1.0% slower than libsecp256k1. With LTO, Ultra wins all ConnectBlock scenarios (+0.9-1.5%).

known_limitations: VerifyScriptP2WPKH: parity only (Ultra 0.4% slower, ranges overlap, inconclusive). Not a regression vs libsecp.
Without LTO: ~0.5-1.0% slower on ConnectBlock — i-cache pressure from larger code footprint. Use LTO for production.
SignTransactionECDSA libsecp had one outlier run (185µs vs ~162-165µs for others) — median is used which limits impact.
_frequency_sensitivity_note: Ultra ConnectBlock performance is sensitive to CPU frequency control. At properly locked 2.5 GHz (cpupower + intel_pstate/no_turbo=1): Ultra wins ECDSA by +1.2% and Schnorr by +0.9% (canonical 2026-05-12 results above). Without cpupower frequency lock (intel_pstate/no_turbo=1 only): Ultra ECDSA appears 7.5% slower, Schnorr 2.5% slower — measurement artifact. libsecp performance is nearly unchanged (~256-257ms) under both conditions, confirming the difference is frequency sensitivity in Ultra's more complex algorithms (ADCX/ADOX, larger i-footprint). Signing advantage is robust: SignSchnorrWithMerkleRoot +18% at both frequency conditions.

results_uncontrolled_frequency_2026_05_1.note: Measurement without cpupower (intel_pstate/no_turbo=1 only, variable 800-2500 MHz). Backend commit: 79363c79. Compiler: GCC 14.2.0 Release+LTO. This is NOT the canonical measurement — use results_lto for performance claims.

ConnectBlockAllEcdsa_ms.verdict: libsecp +7.5% — measurement artifact (no cpupower frequency lock)

ConnectBlockAllSchnorr_ms.verdict: libsecp +2.6% — measurement artifact (no cpupower frequency lock)
